title: DIGEST_INVALID: provided digest did not match uploaded content
body:
I'm not sure what's going on here, and it may be operator error. Apologies in advance if so.  I uploaded an image to GHCR, and one of the layers yields the error `{"errors":[{"code":"DIGEST_INVALID","message":"provided digest did not match uploaded content"}]}`.

```
❯❯❯ skopeo copy --all oci:adam--0.34.0 docker://ghcr.io/brewsci/bio/adam:0.34.0
Getting image list signatures
Copying 2 of 2 images in list
Copying image sha256:5ca32040612f06b498b71c7ab2cbb858e0230e957e73b632dfecc8fe4865caef (1/2)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f6d0f054e4c3 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config f0b692c885 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 225.0b
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Copying image sha256:df9767fd235c278d979d8c845059233e61d077be57215cffe3bc1fec7913f525 (2/2)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob ac202dac0858 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config bae694006e [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 225.0b
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Writing manifest list to image destination
Storing list signatures
```

```console
$ curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer IQ==' https://ghcr.io/v2/brewsci/bio/adam/blobs/sha256:f6d0f054e4c33368eda8f39b60b777fd0a5dc6df585b4e138edf08f5f072c86aa
{"errors":[{"code":"DIGEST_INVALID","message":"provided digest did not match uploaded content"}]}
```

I tried deleting `~/.local/share/containers/cache/blob-info-cache-v1.boltdb` and re-running the `skopeo copy`, and I get the same result. It completes in under four seconds, which seems too fast to have actually uploaded the data. The OCI image and this blob in particular seem fine.

```
$ du -hs adam--0.34.0/blobs/sha256/f6d0f054e4c33368eda8f39b60b777fd0a5dc6df585b4e138edf08f5f072c86a
 45M	adam--0.34.0/blobs/sha256/f6d0f054e4c33368eda8f39b60b777fd0a5dc6df585b4e138edf08f5f072c86a
$ sha256sum adam--0.34.0/blobs/sha256/f6d0f054e4c33368eda8f39b60b777fd0a5dc6df585b4e138edf08f5f072c86a
f6d0f054e4c33368eda8f39b60b777fd0a5dc6df585b4e138edf08f5f072c86a  adam--0.34.0/blobs/sha256/f6d0f054e4c33368eda8f39b60b777fd0a5dc6df585b4e138edf08f5f072c86a
```

The other layer in this same image is fine.
```console
$ curl -sL -H 'Authorization: Bearer IQ==' https://ghcr.io/v2/brewsci/bio/adam/blobs/sha256:ac202dac08582fc68079230223d39839e9588f5d8292dafc7da2e3ac2f8e7eb1 | sha256sum -
ac202dac08582fc68079230223d39839e9588f5d8292dafc7da2e3ac2f8e7eb1  -
$ curl -sL -H 'Authorization: Bearer IQ==' https://ghcr.io/v2/brewsci/bio/adam/blobs/sha256:ac202dac08582fc68079230223d39839e9588f5d8292dafc7da2e3ac2f8e7eb1 | file -
/dev/stdin: gzip compressed data, was "adam-bottle.tar", last modified: Mon Mar 15 14:37:35 2021, from Unix
```

Any tips on where to start troubleshooting would be appreciated. I'm mystified.

comments.body:
Operator error! 😅

There were two problem. Previously GitHub Packages required only `-H 'Authorization: Bearer'` for anonymous download and just today started requiring `-H 'Authorization: Bearer QQ=='`, which was the original source of my error. Then in my test, I copied-and-pasted the SHA-256 incorrectly:

```
$ curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer QQ==' https://ghcr.io/v2/brewsci/bio/adam/blobs/sha256:f6d0f054e4c33368eda8f39b60b777fd0a5dc6df585b4e138edf08f5f072c86aa
{"errors":[{"code":"DIGEST_INVALID","message":"provided digest did not match uploaded content"}]}
$ curl -sL -H 'Authorization: Bearer QQ==' https://ghcr.io/v2/brewsci/bio/adam/blobs/sha256:f6d0f054e4c33368eda8f39b60b777fd0a5dc6df585b4e138edf08f5f072c86a | file -
/dev/stdin: gzip compressed data, was "adam-bottle.tar", last modified: Mon Mar 15 14:37:35 2021, from Unix
```

Sorry for the noise!
